sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Office Application Initiated Network Connection To Non-Local IP
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an office application (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)  that initiate a network connection to a non-private IP addresses.
This rule aims to detect traffic similar to one seen exploited in CVE-2021-42292.
This rule will require an initial baseline and tuning that is specific to your organization.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io', Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton

android.permission.VIBRATE.full_description: Allows the application to control the vibrator.

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.full_description: Allows an application to start itself as soon as the system has finished booting. This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the application to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the information about the status of Wi-Fi.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows an application to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the status of all networks.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description: Allows an application to create network sockets.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows an application to write to the SD card.

android.permission.FLASHLIGHT.full_description: Allows the application to control the flashlight.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Session Traversal Utilities for NAT (STUN Binding Request On Non-Standard High Port)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious DNS Query for IP Lookup Service APIs
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects DNS queries for IP lookup services such as "api.ipify.org" originating from a non browser process.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Python Image Load By Non-Python Process
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the image load of "Python Core" by a non-Python process. This might be indicative of a execution of executable that has been bundled from Python code.
Various tools like Py2Exe, PyInstaller, and cx_Freeze are used to bundle Python code into standalone executables.
Threat actors often use these tools to bundle malicious Python scripts into executables, sometimes to obfuscate the code or to bypass security measures.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Patrick St. John, OTR (Open Threat Research)

attributes.magic: data

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: INDICATOR-SHELLCODE x86 inc ebx NOOP
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert ip $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any ( msg:"INDICATOR-SHELLCODE x86 inc ebx NOOP"; content:"CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC"; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop,ruleset community; classtype:shellcode-detect; sid:1390; rev:17; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: PROTOCOL-ICMP PATH MTU denial of service attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert icmp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HOME_NET any ( msg:"PROTOCOL-ICMP PATH MTU denial of service attempt"; itype:3; icode:4; byte_test:2,<,576,2; metadata:policy max-detect-ips drop; reference:bugtraq,13124; reference:cve,2004-1060; classtype:attempted-dos; sid:3626; rev:9; )

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: FILEEXT JPG file claimed

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: FILE tracking GIF (1x1 pixel)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: SURICATA STREAM reassembly overlap with different data

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: SURICATA HTTP compression bomb
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"SURICATA HTTP compression bomb"; flow:established; app-layer-event:http.compression_bomb; flowint:http.anomaly.count,+,1; classtype:protocol-command-decode; sid:2221049; rev:1;)

android.permission.VIBRATE.full_description: Allows the application to control the vibrator.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows an application to read from external storage

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.full_description: Allows an application to start itself as soon as the system has finished booting. This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the application to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the information about the status of Wi-Fi.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows an application to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description: Allows an application to view the status of all networks.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description: Allows an application to create network sockets.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows an application to write to the SD card.

android.permission.FLASHLIGHT.full_description: Allows the application to control the flashlight.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects an Adobe Type 1 Font. The Type 1 Font Format is a standardized font format for digital imaging applications.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious New Instance Of An Office COM Object
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects an svchost process spawning an instance of an office application. This happens when the initial word application creates an instance of one of the Office COM objects such as 'Word.Application', 'Excel.Application', etc.
This can be used by malicious actors to create malicious Office documents with macros on the fly. (See vba2clr project in the references)

trid.file_type: Word Microsoft Office Open XML Format document

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.short_description: access approximate location only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.full_description: This app can get your approximate location from location services while the app is in use. Location services for your device must be turned on for the app to get location.

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.full_description:
Allows the app to
        have itself started as soon as the system has finished booting.
        This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the
        app to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view information
      about Wi-Fi networking, such as whether Wi-Fi is enabled and name of
      connected Wi-Fi devices.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description:
Allows the app to create
     network sockets and use custom network protocols. The browser and other
     applications provide means to send data to the internet, so this
     permission is not required to send data to the internet.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.short_description: access precise location only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.full_description: This app can get your precise location from location services while the app is in use. Location services for your device must be turned on for the app to get location. This may increase battery usage.

android.permission.REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKA.full_description: Allows an application to request installation of packages.

android.permission.USE_FINGERPRINT.full_description: Allows the app to use fingerprint hardware for authentication

android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_SY.short_description: run foreground service with the type systemExempted
android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_SY.full_description: Allows the app to make use of foreground services with the type systemExempted

android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE.short_description: detect screen captures of app windows
android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE.full_description: This app will get notified when a screenshot is taken while the app is in use.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view
      information about network connections such as which networks exist and are
      connected.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.short_description: modify or delete the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows the app to write the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS.full_description: Allows the app to show notifications

android.permission.USE_BIOMETRIC.full_description: Allows the app to use biometric hardware for authentication

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.short_description: read the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows the app to read the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES.full_description: Allows an app to see all installed packages.

android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE.full_description: Allows the app to make use of foreground services.

android.permission.CAMERA.full_description: This app can take pictures and record videos using the camera while the app is in use.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows the app to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects the presence of a number of Windows API functionality often seen within embedded executables. crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects suspicious metadata within a Microsoft Word document. Document properties, also known as metadata, are details about a file that describe or identify it. Document properties include details such as title, author name, operator, subject, and keywords that identify the document's topic or contents.

attributes.magic: ASCII text, with very long lines (556u), with CRLF line terminators

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects base64 encoded Powershell directives.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature fires on the presence of Base64 encoded URI prefixes (http:// and https://) across any file. The simple presence of such strings is not inherently an indicator of malicious content, but is worth further investigation.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects content that indicates the presence of August malware.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects the presence of a number of Windows API functionality often seen within embedded executables. When this signature alerts on an executable, it is not an indication of malicious behavior. However, if seen firing in other file types, deeper investigation may be warranted.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects suspicious metadata within a Microsoft Word document. Document properties, also known as metadata, are details about a file that describe or identify it. Document properties include details such as title, author name, operator, subject, and keywords that identify the document's topic or contents.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs
